skill.name: review-changes
skill.description: Review current git changes, compare them with related plans or issues, and produce a structured code review focused on bugs, quality, and scope.
skill.summary: Review repository changes against plans, requirements, and engineering quality standards.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples, file extensions, and review templates. Legitimate medium risk remains because the skill inspects repository files, runs git, queries GitHub issues, and writes review reports.

medium_findings.title: Repository and Issue Data Access Required
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to inspect git changes, read related plan files, and optionally query GitHub issues. This is appropriate for code review, but it can expose repository content and issue metadata to the active assistant session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require git inspection, plan-file reading, and optional GitHub issue lookup. These actions are legitimate for the skill but create clear data-access risk.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Findings Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external-command detections occur in Markdown code fences or checklist instructions for git status, git diff, and report generation. I did not find evidence of command injection, dynamic shell construction, or hidden command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible Markdown guidance, not executable code. The commands are standard git review commands with no interpolated untrusted input shown.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts appear to match Markdown filenames, prose, and template text rather than actual cryptographic operations. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, encryption, hashing APIs, or credential handling in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation or use of weak algorithms. The static hits align with common false matches in Markdown content.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance alert points to review guidance about verifying removed code. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, persistence, or credential discovery instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss plan compliance for code removal. The context does not match system reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Code changes can drift from requirements or introduce subtle bugs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured review of diffs, plans, and quality risks.
actual_capabilities: Inspects staged and unstaged git diffs to identify changed files.
Compares implementation details with related plans, issues, or requirements.
Categorizes findings by severity and engineering concern.
Checks whether required removals and task coverage are complete.
Produces a structured review report with line-specific findings.
Summarizes merge readiness with approve or request-changes guidance.
limitations: It depends on repository access and accurate git state.
It does not prove runtime behavior without tests or execution.
It may need user guidance when multiple plans or issues could apply.
It should not modify source files during the review.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge review for developers
use_cases.description: Run a structured review before opening or updating a pull request.

use_cases.title: Plan compliance check for tech leads
use_cases.description: Compare implementation work against a written plan or issue requirements.

use_cases.title: Quality audit for maintainers
use_cases.description: Find bugs, missing edge cases, and unnecessary complexity in incoming changes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Diff Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of current local changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current git changes and summarize any bugs, quality issues, and merge blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Plan-Based Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare work against a known implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current changes against the related plan in .plans. Flag missing requirements, scope creep, and architecture drift.

prompt_templates.title: PR Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare changes for a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review staged and unstaged changes for pull request readiness. Focus on correctness, tests, edge cases, and maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Strict Release Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before release or merge freeze.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a strict review of all changed files. Verify task coverage, removal requirements, security impact, and cross-file consistency.

output_examples.input: Review my current git changes before I open a pull request.
output_examples.output: Assessment: Request changes. Two high-priority issues need fixes, including missing error handling and incomplete test coverage. The core approach matches the plan.

output_examples.input: Check whether this implementation matches the plan.
output_examples.output: Coverage is incomplete. Three requirements were verified, one requirement is missing, and one removal item still has legacy references.
The review highlights one unnecessary abstraction and one duplicated helper. A simpler shared function would reduce maintenance risk.

best_practices: Run the review after tests and formatting so findings focus on behavior and design.
Provide the related plan, issue number, or pull request context when available.
Review the generated findings before sharing them with teammates.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for automated tests or security scanning.
Do not ask it to approve changes when requirements are incomplete.
Do not run it in repositories that contain secrets without proper safeguards.

faq.question: Does this skill edit my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the assistant to review changes and report findings, not modify source files.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is intended for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that can inspect repository files and git state.

faq.question: Can it compare changes to a plan?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks the assistant to look for related plan files or issue references when available.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: Only optional GitHub issue lookup may use network access through the GitHub CLI.

faq.question: What kind of issues can it find?
faq.answer: It focuses on bugs, missing requirements, over-engineering, poor patterns, test gaps, and plan deviations.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk notice because it needs repository access and may run git or GitHub CLI commands.
